# Transcrição: What_Do_Magicians_&_Casinos_Have_In_Common_

- **Fonte:** What_Do_Magicians_&_Casinos_Have_In_Common_
- **Duração:** 02:12
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:12]

Last night I got to spend time with Scott Stratton and Scott Monty, two awesome speakers here in America. They took me through the Venetian hotel and casino. Belizmo! One of the great things about Vegas is everything here is so extreme, right? Everything is pushed to the edge of its potential. Everything here is so just over the top. But that's what makes Vegas so magical. There's a great quote by a magician, Teller, and he says that magic is just someone spending more time on something than anybody would reasonably expect. Some of the hotels here spend more time on the reception design. Some of them have a fish tank behind it. They spend more time on that than anybody would reasonably expect. And that's why when you come to these hotels, it feels like magic. So it just makes you think, I mean, what if you applied some of these philosophies into your own life? Where could you spend more time than anybody would reasonably expect in on your life, in your career, that would make it magic so that you would get the same results that these grand big hotels get here and the magicians get when they perform magic. Every single casino here, they compete with one another on different things. It's just incredible because none of them compete on the same thing. They're all the same thing. They're all about gambling. They're all about a hotel, but they all try to create themselves a unique value proposition. The message that I really want to share with you here is that whether you're a financial advisor, you're a plumber, you're an electrician, you're a nurse, you're a doctor, even though you do similar things to other people in your field, you don't have to be the same. You can be different. And the way to discover your unique value proposition is you spending more time than anybody else would reasonably expect on what makes you different. If you do the work and you spend the time and you discover what makes you unique, then you don't compete against anyone. The most extravagant place I've ever been, this place, is crazy.
